run_summary: Portfolio down modestly (3 of 4 positions underwater). SHAK thesis intact and in profit—hold. TXRH and DCO deteriorating on sector/macro pressure; both hold pending their near-term catalysts. AEIS re-entry flagged at conf 3 (OVERDONE) after 4-day loss; re-entry bar unmet (new flag is still conf 3, exit reasoning already cited sector beta as thesis-killer). New flag pool contains 1 exploratory candidate (BLDR, UNDERDONE conf 3 with named 8-K combo) and 4 rational/low-confidence names (KTOS, BBIO, AMR all RATIONAL conf 3; DCO is a duplicate already held). AEIS re-entry rejected; BLDR merits exploratory position test.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Today's 8-K with financing signature (1.01/2.03/3.02) on top of ongoing semicap sector pressure
entries.thesis: AEIS filed an 8-K today with items 1.01 (material agreement), 2.03 (financial obligation creation), and 3.02 (unregistered equity sale) — this signature is a convertible-note or financing deal, not a guide cut. The stock is being lumped in with the broader semicap selloff (ONTO, ENTG, VECO all down similarly) on the back of the Seagate memory-capex commentary, but the company-specific catalyst is a capital-structure event whose dilution/coupon terms could easily be priced in or even bullish if proceeds fund accretive use. The five-day drawdown is steep relative to sector peers and likely overshoots if the financing terms are reasonable.
entries.what_kills: 8-K reveals deeply dilutive equity issuance at a discount, or proceeds earmarked to cover an operational shortfall; semicap selloff intensifies on further negative AI-capex commentary
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies. AEIS closed 4 days ago at -6.48% realized loss; prior thesis was killed because the decline was pure semicap-sector beta (1.6x SMH), not an idiosyncratic catalyst. New flag re-lists as OVERDONE conf 3 with revised narrative: financing structure (1.01/2.03/3.02 combo indicates convertible or structured finance) and the thesis that convert terms could be favorable/priced-in. However, re-entry bar is NOT met on two counts: (1) confidence is still 3, not ≥4 (execution layer will downgrade to WATCH anyway); (2) NO genuinely new information—the 8-K itself (5/13 filing with same 1.01/2.03/3.02 items) was the day-one catalyst I already cited on exit, and the flag is simply re-describing the same event with added thesis flavor about financing terms. The prior exit reasoning explicitly rejected sector-beta narratives as non-tradeable; flag does not introduce new information to override that. Reject re-entry and WATCH instead, pending either a sector stabilization (which would directly confirm prior thesis-kill) or an explicit announcement of convert terms + use of proceeds.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
entries.catalyst: Iran geopolitical escalation lifting defense sector
entries.thesis: KTOS's 5/15 8-K is item 5.07 (shareholder vote results) — annual meeting boilerplate, not a catalyst. Today's move is straightforward: ITA defense ETF +1.4%, Trump's Iran posture is escalatory (postponed strike at allies' request, with surrender rhetoric on Truth Social), and KTOS is a high-beta unmanned-systems play within defense. Magnitude is appropriate given its typical sector beta of ~2-3x ITA on geopolitical days.
entries.what_kills: Iran de-escalates suddenly; defense ETF rolls over and KTOS holds bid — would suggest unseen company catalyst worth investigating
entries.reasoning: KTOS flagged as RATIONAL conf 3 on Iran escalation lifting defense sector (ITA +1.36%). The setup is mechanical and well-described: 5/15 8-K is annual-meeting boilerplate (item 5.07), no idiosyncratic catalyst; today's +4.09% move is pure geopolitical beta to ITA on Trump-Iran escalation rhetoric. Thesis is correct but not edge-generating—a RATIONAL conf 3 call that says 'the move is justified by sector tailwinds' is not an argument to buy at today's prices. The five_day_change_pct is -4.86%, meaning the stock is actually down over the week despite today's rally; this suggests the geopolitical bid may be late in the intraday. No named catalyst beyond macro sentiment. Skip—this is sector momentum, not a discovery worthy of capital allocation.

entries.name: Ducommun Incorporated
entries.catalyst: Defense sector pullback after positive earnings reaction faded
entries.thesis: DCO reported earnings 5/12 (2.02 8-K) and was actually +4.75% over 5 days going into today, suggesting an initial positive reaction. Today's -5.17% on average volume with ITA -3.2% looks like sector-sympathy giving back the post-earnings pop. With volume only 1.04x, this is not a fundamental re-rating — but the give-back may have further to run if defense weakness persists, because the post-earnings bid was the only thing supporting it.
entries.what_kills: If DCO holds the pre-earnings reference and defense ETF stabilizes, the give-back is complete and thesis breaks
entries.reasoning: DCO is flagged as a new UNDERDONE conf 3 discovery, but this ticker is already in portfolio with 4 shares at cost 149.01, currently down -4.93%. The flag describes post-earnings drift on sector weakness, days horizon. This is NOT a new discovery—it is the position I already assessed above (hold for defense stabilization). Reject the duplicate flag entry.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.thesis: The 5/13 8-K (item 8.01 'other events') filed today is the proximate driver — typically used for clinical data updates, label expansions, or partnership disclosures. Combined with the 5/8 material agreement filing (item 1.01 + 1.02), there's a sequence of business development activity. +4% on average volume is a measured reaction — neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.
entries.what_kills: If it's just an investor-day presentation re-filing, +4% is the full reaction
entries.reasoning: BBIO flagged as RATIONAL conf 3 on 8-K cluster (earnings 5/7, material agreement 5/8, other event 5/13). The setup is boilerplate: 5/13 8.01 filing (other events) is proximate driver, move is +4.03% on 1.06x volume, described as 'measured'. The flag explicitly acknowledges the move is 'neither overdone nor underdone' and that the thesis depends on reading the actual 8.01 exhibit (clinical data update vs. routine presentation). This is a RATIONAL confidence 3 flag—the move is justified given sector/event context, with no clear directional edge. No specific catalyst URL provided in the flag description (the URL given is the SEC filing link, not a news catalyst). Conviction bar not met; confidence is too low and thesis is too speculative (depends on undisclosed 8.01 content). Skip.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.thesis: Continued post-earnings selling after the 5/8 print (item 2.02) and Reg FD disclosure. Met coal pricing remains under pressure and the cumulative -9% five-day move plus today's -4.6% is consistent with a soft print followed by analyst cuts. Magnitude appropriate for a commodity producer in a weak pricing environment.
entries.what_kills: China stimulus announcement during Trump-Xi summit lifting steel/met coal expectations could sharply reverse this
entries.reasoning: AMR flagged as RATIONAL conf 3 on post-earnings drift in met coal name (5/8 earnings, -9% five-day move plus -4.57% today). The setup is sector-driven: met coal pricing weak, earnings likely soft, analyst cuts underway. Flag acknowledges this is post-earnings selling momentum, not a surprise. The five-day move is -9% (not available in data layer, cited in prose) and today -4.57%, consistent with soft cyclical earnings in a weak commodity environment. Thesis is RATIONAL (move is justified by weak fundamentals) not a trading edge. The 'what_to_learn' note about Trump-Xi summit upside is interesting but is not the basis for a today trade. Confidence 3, RATIONAL classification, no contrarian edge. Skip.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K item 8.01 (other events) filed May 18; multi-day decline on elevated volume
entries.thesis: ONTO filed an 8.01 'other events' 8-K which is the lowest-signal item code, and the stock has continued lower over several sessions on heavy volume (4x average). Without the exhibit content, I cannot tell if this is a guide-down disclosure, a customer-loss notice, or boilerplate. SMH is only modestly down today, so this is company-specific, but I cannot articulate a confident direction either way from item 8.01 alone.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Defensive sector rotation + technical bounce; 5.07 filing is non-substantive
entries.thesis: RGEN's 8-K is item 5.07 (typically annual meeting voting results), which is administrative and not a catalyst. The move is consistent with XLV +1.1% defensive rotation and a bounce off a recent multi-day decline. No clear mispricing — magnitude is appropriate given sector rotation and recent weakness.
entries.what_kills: 8-K exhibit contains something other than vote results (unlikely for 5.07-only)

entries.name: SkyWest, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; likely sympathy with oil-price-driven airline weakness
entries.thesis: SKYW has continued lower over multiple sessions with no 8-K, no recent or upcoming earnings, and on below-average volume. Most likely driver is jet fuel pressure from XLE/oil bid on Iran tensions and broader risk-off cyclical selling, but with no idiosyncratic signal I can't make a directional call at conf 3+.

entries.name: Veracyte
entries.catalyst: Unknown — empty catalyst signals, against-tape move in healthcare diagnostics
entries.thesis: VCYT up nearly 8% with 1.4x volume but catalyst_signals is empty — no SEC filing, no earnings window. XBI is down 2.3% so this is not biotech sympathy; it's idiosyncratic and against the tape. Could be a sell-side upgrade, conference presentation reception, M&A rumor, or guideline-inclusion news that didn't surface in the news feed. Without visible evidence I cannot articulate either a fade or follow-through case at confidence 3+.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: BEAM, CRSP, BBIO all down 4-5% with XBI -3% — small/mid biotech is duration-sensitive and 30Y at 5.1% (highest in a year) plus Fed hike repricing crushes discount-rate-sensitive names. No catalyst signals on BEAM. 5-day -13.6% indicates ongoing sector unwind. Magnitude is roughly 1.7x XBI which is normal beta for a clinical-stage gene editing name.
entries.what_kills: If a positive clinical readout or M&A headline hits BEAM specifically, thesis breaks

entries.name: WEX Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K with annual meeting vote (5.07) + unidentified 8.01 exhibit; possible PE/activist chatter
entries.thesis: WEX +5.5% with the tape down 1.2% and on AVERAGE volume (0.96x) is genuinely odd. The 8-K is shareholder vote results — that doesn't drive a 5% rally. Could be activist filing, takeout chatter (Magnum PE takeover news today might be reviving fintech buyout speculation), or a buyback authorization in the 8.01 exhibit. Without seeing the 8.01 content I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+: a buyback/strategic review would be UNDERDONE bullish, a vote-driven mechanical bid would be OVERDONE.

entries.name: Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated
entries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; XLRE weak but magnitude exceeds sector move materially
entries.thesis: Move is the continuation of a multi-day decline in a weak sub-sector (XLRE -0.7% on a risk-on tape), but a 7% single-day drop on 0.9x volume with no filing is unusual — either a delayed reaction to the prior earnings print, an analyst action, or news not yet in our feed. I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+ without identifying the trigger.
entries.what_kills: If no specific catalyst surfaces and the stock stabilizes tomorrow, then this was just illiquid continuation selling and the gap fades

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Possibly Trump-Xi trade optimism + cyclicals reflation; no SAIA-specific filing
entries.thesis: Trump-Xi summit + Boeing/China order headlines are bullish for goods-trade-exposed industrials, and LTL is a leading freight indicator. But XLI is only +0.5% and SAIA's move is 13x the sector — either a peer (ODFL, XPO) reported something positive, a freight-volume data print landed, or there's a SAIA-specific item not in our feed. Cannot commit to a direction without identifying it.
entries.what_kills: If no peer/industry catalyst is identifiable, the move is likely a squeeze on the trade-thaw narrative and reverses if China headlines disappoint

entries.name: Extreme Networks
entries.catalyst: Possibly AI-networking sympathy with AVGO; no company-specific filing
entries.thesis: Plausibly riding AI-networking sympathy (AVGO +5.5%, ANET-adjacent demand narrative), but +8.5% on 1.37x volume with no filing typically indicates either an upgrade, a contract win not yet in our feed, or speculative pre-positioning. Direction unclear without identifying the trigger.
entries.what_kills: If trigger turns out to be a rumor or speculative AI-tag, fade risk is high once the broader semis rally cools

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Likely sympathy with KTOS; Anduril valuation overhang
entries.thesis: Co-moving with KTOS on what appears to be Anduril competitive concerns. Volume only 1.14x suggests this is sympathy, not company-specific news. Direction unclear without knowing the actual driver.
entries.what_kills: AVAV-specific bad news in an 8-K would change this from sympathy to fundamental

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filings or news
entries.thesis: Sub-1x volume on a -6.9% move is unusual — suggests a thin tape selloff rather than institutional distribution, possibly from a specific seller or a quiet downgrade. Five-day -8.9% indicates this is continuation of a slide, not a single-day event. Without identifying the driver I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If volume picks up to 2x+ on next session with a fundamental catalyst, the low-volume read is wrong

entries.name: Axsome Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.thesis: Sub-1x volume on a +5% move suggests short-covering or thin tape rather than institutional accumulation. With no visible catalyst and XBI only +0.7%, this is hard to attribute. Could be Auvelity script-data positive read or pipeline rumor, but I can't construct a conf 3+ directional case.
entries.what_kills: Reversal on next session with no news suggests technical-only move
